public_api.name: slugify
public_api.description: Converts human-readable text into a lowercase URL-safe slug string.

requirements.description: The slugify function must accept a single parameter 'text' of type str.
The slugify function must return a string representing a lowercase URL-safe slug.
All alphabetic characters in the input text must be converted to lowercase in the output slug.
Leading and trailing whitespace in the input text must be ignored in the output slug.
One or more whitespace characters in the input text must be replaced with a single hyphen in the output slug.
All punctuation characters must be removed from the input text in the output slug.
Existing hyphens in the input text must be preserved in the output slug.
Multiple adjacent hyphens in the output slug must collapse to a single hyphen.
If the input text is an empty string, the output slug must be an empty string.
If the input text contains only whitespace characters, the output slug must be an empty string.
If the input text contains only punctuation characters, the output slug must be an empty string.
If the input text is already a slugified string, the output must be the same string.
The slugify function must raise a TypeError if the input parameter 'text' is not a string.
The slugify function must handle ASCII characters only.
The slugify function must have no side effects such as reading or writing files, making network requests, or executing external commands.
The slugify function must run in O(N) time complexity and use O(N) space complexity, where N is the length of the input string.
